column2:
make a decision
NOTE: "do a decision" salah — gunakan "make".
The government has made a decision to ban single-use plastics nationwide.
Students often need to make difficult decisions about their future careers.

make an effort
NOTE: "do an effort" salah — gunakan "make".
Citizens must make a greater effort to reduce household waste.
She made every effort to improve her English before the exam.

make progress
NOTE: "do progress" salah — gunakan "make".
Developing countries have made significant progress in reducing poverty.
He is making steady progress in his language studies.

make a difference
NOTE: "do a difference" salah — gunakan "make".
Small lifestyle changes can make a real difference to the environment.
Education makes a profound difference in people's lives.

make sense
NOTE: "have sense" salah — gunakan "make sense".
It makes sense to invest in renewable energy for long-term sustainability.
His argument does not make sense given the current evidence.

make a contribution
NOTE: "give a contribution" sering salah — "make" lebih natural.
Volunteers make a valuable contribution to society.
Renewable energy makes a significant contribution to reducing emissions.

make a mistake
NOTE: "do a mistake" salah — gunakan "make".
It is common to make mistakes when learning a new language.
The company made a costly mistake by ignoring market trends.

make friends
NOTE: "do friends" salah — gunakan "make".
Children typically make friends easily at school.
Studying abroad gave her the chance to make friends from many cultures.

do research
NOTE: "make research" salah — gunakan "do" atau "conduct".
Scientists are doing research into renewable energy sources.
Universities do extensive research on climate change.

do homework
NOTE: "make homework" salah — gunakan "do".
Students should do their homework regularly to reinforce learning.
He always does his homework before watching television.

do business
NOTE: "make business" salah — gunakan "do".
Many international companies do business in Southeast Asia.
It is difficult to do business without trust between partners.

do harm
NOTE: "make harm" salah — gunakan "do" atau "cause".
Excessive screen time can do harm to children's eyesight.
Pollution does irreversible harm to marine ecosystems.

do a favor
NOTE: "make a favor" salah — gunakan "do".
Could you do me a favor and review my essay?
She did her colleague a favor by covering his shift.

do your best
NOTE: "make your best" salah — gunakan "do".
Candidates are encouraged to do their best on the IELTS examination.
He always does his best regardless of the difficulty.

take action
NOTE: "make action" salah — gunakan "take".
Governments must take immediate action to address climate change.
The authorities took swift action against the offenders.

take advantage of
NOTE: "use advantage" salah — gunakan "take advantage of".
Students should take advantage of free online learning resources.
Many businesses took advantage of the new tax incentives.

take into account
NOTE: sinonim formal: "consider", "take into consideration".
Policymakers must take environmental impact into account when planning.
The judge took her age into account when delivering the verdict.

take responsibility
NOTE: "have responsibility" lebih lemah — "take" menunjukkan tindakan aktif.
Individuals must take responsibility for their carbon footprint.
The minister took full responsibility for the policy failure.

take place
NOTE: "happen" lebih informal — "take place" lebih formal/IELTS.
The conference will take place in Jakarta next March.
Significant changes have taken place in the education system.

take part in
NOTE: sinonim: "participate in".
Over 200 students took part in the environmental campaign.
Citizens are encouraged to take part in local elections.

take a break
NOTE: "make a break" salah — gunakan "take".
It is important to take regular breaks while studying for IELTS.
Workers should take a break every two hours to remain productive.

take notes
NOTE: "make notes" juga benar tapi "take notes" lebih umum.
Students should take detailed notes during lectures.
She took careful notes throughout the seminar.

have an impact
NOTE: "give impact" salah — gunakan "have an impact on".
Social media has a profound impact on teenagers' self-esteem.
The new policy will have a significant impact on small businesses.

have an effect
NOTE: "give effect" salah — gunakan "have an effect on".
Air pollution has a detrimental effect on respiratory health.
The reform had little effect on student performance.

have an opportunity
NOTE: "get an opportunity" juga benar; "have" lebih umum di IELTS.
Students have the opportunity to study abroad through scholarships.
She had a rare opportunity to meet the prime minister.

have access to
NOTE: gunakan preposisi "to", bukan "for".
Many rural communities still do not have access to clean water.
Students should have access to high-quality education regardless of background.

have an influence on
NOTE: gunakan preposisi "on", bukan "to".
Parents have a strong influence on their children's values.
Western media has had a considerable influence on Asian pop culture.

have a tendency
NOTE: diikuti "to + verb".
Young people have a tendency to spend excessive time on social media.
Politicians have a tendency to make unrealistic promises.

give priority to
NOTE: alternatif: "prioritize".
The government should give priority to education and healthcare.
Companies must give priority to employee well-being.

give rise to
NOTE: formal sinonim "cause"; sangat IELTS-style.
Rapid urbanisation has given rise to numerous social problems.
The new technology gave rise to ethical concerns.

give an example
NOTE: "make an example" maknanya beda.
Could you give an example to support your argument?
The teacher gave several examples to clarify the concept.

give attention to
NOTE: alternatif lebih umum: "pay attention to".
Schools should give greater attention to mental health issues.
Researchers have given little attention to this aspect of the problem.

give consideration
NOTE: formal — sering "give careful/serious consideration to".
The committee gave careful consideration to all proposals.
Authorities should give serious consideration to environmental impact.

pay attention
NOTE: "give attention" juga benar tapi "pay attention" lebih natural.
Students must pay close attention to grammar in IELTS Writing.
The media pays excessive attention to celebrity scandals.

pay a visit
NOTE: formal — "visit" sebagai verb lebih umum sehari-hari.
The minister paid a visit to the disaster-affected region.
We paid a visit to my grandparents last weekend.

pay tribute
NOTE: diikuti "to" + person/thing.
The nation paid tribute to the soldiers who lost their lives.
Colleagues paid tribute to her decades of dedicated service.

pay the price
NOTE: idiomatik — menanggung konsekuensi.
Future generations will pay the price for today's environmental neglect.
She paid the price for her dishonesty when she lost the job.

carry out (research)
NOTE: formal sinonim "conduct"; lebih akademik dari "do".
Scientists carried out extensive research on the new vaccine.
The team will carry out a survey across five provinces.

bring about (change)
NOTE: formal sinonim "cause"; sangat IELTS Writing.
Technology has brought about dramatic changes in education.
The reforms brought about significant improvements in healthcare.

come up with (an idea)
NOTE: tidak terpisahkan; partikel selalu bersama.
Researchers came up with an innovative solution to the problem.
She came up with a brilliant idea for the marketing campaign.

put forward (a proposal)
NOTE: formal sinonim "propose", "suggest".
The committee put forward several proposals to reduce inequality.
He put forward a compelling argument for educational reform.

deal with (a problem)
NOTE: sinonim: "address", "tackle", "handle".
Governments must deal with the issue of plastic pollution urgently.
Schools need better strategies to deal with bullying.

figure out (a solution)
NOTE: agak informal — "work out" lebih netral di IELTS Writing.
Engineers figured out a solution to the recurring system failure.
It took her hours to figure out the answer.

look into (investigate)
NOTE: sinonim: "investigate", "examine".
The authorities are looking into allegations of corruption.
Researchers looked into the causes of declining biodiversity.

point out (note)
NOTE: sinonim formal: "indicate", "highlight".
The report points out that emissions have risen sharply since 2000.
She pointed out several flaws in the methodology.

set up (establish)
NOTE: sinonim formal: "establish", "found".
The government has set up a task force to combat cybercrime.
They set up a non-profit organisation to support refugees.

break down (analyze/fail)
NOTE: dua makna: (1) menganalisis komponen, (2) rusak/gagal.
The analyst broke down the data into three categories.
Negotiations broke down after both sides refused to compromise.

turn out (result)
NOTE: diikuti "to be" + adjective atau "that" clause.
The new policy turned out to be highly effective.
It turned out that the rumours were entirely false.

take over
NOTE: bisa "take over a company" atau "take over from someone".
A multinational corporation took over the local firm last year.
She took over as director after her predecessor retired.

get over
NOTE: makna: pulih dari sakit, kekecewaan, atau pengalaman buruk.
It took him weeks to get over the flu.
She struggled to get over the loss of her job.

run out of
NOTE: selalu diikuti "of" + noun.
The world is rapidly running out of fossil fuels.
We have run out of time to complete the project.

look forward to
NOTE: diikuti noun atau verb-ing, BUKAN to-infinitive.
I am looking forward to receiving your reply.
Students look forward to the summer holidays every year.

strong / weak argument
NOTE: hindari "big argument" untuk konteks akademik.
She presented a strong argument in favour of renewable energy.
The proposal was rejected because it rested on a weak argument.

key factor
NOTE: sinonim: "crucial factor", "main factor".
Education is a key factor in reducing poverty.
Climate change has become a key factor in global migration patterns.

vital role
NOTE: sinonim: "crucial role", "essential role"; diikuti "in + verb-ing".
Teachers play a vital role in shaping young minds.
Forests play a vital role in regulating the global climate.

profound impact
NOTE: sinonim: "significant impact", "major impact"; diikuti "on".
The pandemic had a profound impact on global education systems.
Industrialisation has had a profound impact on the environment.

widespread belief
NOTE: sinonim: "common belief", "popular belief".
There is a widespread belief that exercise improves mental health.
Despite widespread belief to the contrary, the data suggests otherwise.

growing concern
NOTE: sinonim: "increasing concern", "rising concern".
There is growing concern about youth unemployment.
Cybersecurity has become a growing concern for businesses worldwide.

significant difference
NOTE: sinonim: "considerable difference", "marked difference".
There is a significant difference between the two groups' performance.
Vaccination has made a significant difference to public health.

common misconception
NOTE: pola: "It is a common misconception that..."
It is a common misconception that learning a language is only easy for children.
A common misconception about IELTS is that vocabulary alone determines the score.
